content.text: Do seven 7 and only seven of the following.

problem.text: Let \(X\) be a set, and let \(\mathcal T\) be the collection of all subsets \(A\) of \(X\) such that \(X-A\) is either finite or all of \(X\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that \((X,\mathcal T)\) is topological space.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Now let \(X=\mathbb R\), the real line.

subparts.label: (i)
subparts.text: Determine if the sequence \(x_n=7n+8\) converges and if so, to which point or points.

subparts.label: (ii)
subparts.text: Show that every infinite set is connected.

problem.text: Let \(X\) be a topological space. Let \(D=\{(x,y)\in X\times X:x=y\}\). Prove that \(X\) is a Hausdorff space if and only if \(D\) is a closed subset of \(X\times X\), where \(X\times X\) has the product topology.
Show that \(\mathbb R^{\mathbb Z_+}\) with the uniform topology is not connected.

A chain of subsets is a collection \(A_n\) for \(n\in\mathbb Z_+\) with the property that for all \(i\), \(A_i\cap A_{i+1}\ne\varnothing\). If each \(A_n\) in a chain is connected, show that \(\bigcup_{n\in\mathbb Z_+}A_n\) is also connected.
If \(A\) is a countable set, show that \(\mathbb R^2-A\) is path connected.
A space is called locally Baire if every point has an open neighborhood that is a Baire space. Prove that locally Baire implies Baire.
Show that the Tietze Extension Theorem implies Urysohn’s Lemma.
Assume that \((X,d)\) is metric. Show the following

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: If \(X\) is compact then it is second countable
